adopted_child_of.description: Indicates that the subject was legally or ritually adopted by the object as their child.

adoptive_parent_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the adoptive mother or father of the object.

ally_of.description: Indicates a formal or informal alliance between the subject and object, typically in conflict or warfare.

ancestor_of.description: Indicates that the subject is a forebear of the object across any number of generations.

associated_with.description: Specifies a general or thematic relationship between the subject and the object.

born_in.description: Specifies the location or realm where the subject was born.

brother_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the male sibling of the object.

buried_in.description: Specifies the location where the subject is entombed or buried.

child_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the offspring or descendant of the object.

cousin_of.description: Indicates that the subject and object are children of siblings, sharing a common grandparent.
cousin_of.derivation_rule: If A → child_of → B, C → child_of → D, and B → sibling_of → D, then A → cousin_of → C

creates.description: Indicates that the subject authored, forged, or brought the object into existence.

daughter_in_law_of.description: Indicates that the subject is married to the object's son.

daughter_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the female offspring of the object.

defeats.description: Indicates that the subject conquered or bested the object in a conflict.

deity_of.description: Indicates that the subject is a divine figure representing or ruling the object.

descendant_of.description: Indicates that the subject is descended from the object across any number of generations.

derived_from.description: Indicates that the subject's name, origin, or nature originates from the object.

dies_in.description: Specifies the location or circumstance in which the subject died.

disciple_of.description: Indicates that the subject is a devoted follower or student of the object, typically in religious or philosophical context.

disciples_of.description: Indicates that the object is a devoted follower or student of the subject.

enemy_of.description: Indicates active hostility or opposition between the subject and object.

exile_by.description: Indicates that the subject was banished or exiled by the object.

founds.description: Indicates that the subject established or created a location, group, or institution.

friend_of.description: Indicates a friendly or companionable relationship between the subject and object.

god_of.description: Indicates that the male or gender-neutral subject is the divine ruler or embodiment of the object.

goddess_of.description: Indicates that the female subject is the divine ruler or embodiment of the object.

grandchild_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the child of the object's child.
grandchild_of.derivation_rule: If A → child_of → B and B → child_of → C, then A → grandchild_of → C

grandparent_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the parent of the object's parent.
grandparent_of.derivation_rule: If A → parent_of → B and B → parent_of → C, then A → grandparent_of → C

guardian_of.description: Indicates that the subject has legal or moral responsibility for the care and protection of the object.

half_sibling_of.description: Indicates that the subject and object share exactly one parent.
half_sibling_of.derivation_rule: If A → child_of → B, C → child_of → B, and A and C have different other parents, then A → half_sibling_of → C

identified_with.description: Indicates that the subject is synonymous with, or heavily equated to, the object.

in_law_of.description: Indicates that the subject and object are related through marriage (general in-law relationship).

lives_in.description: Specifies the location or realm where the subject resides.

located_in.description: Specifies the geographical or physical region where the subject is found.

lord_of.description: Indicates that the subject holds feudal or hierarchical dominion over the object.

lover_of.description: Indicates a romantic or sexual relationship distinct from formal marriage.

mother_in_law_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the mother of the object's spouse.

mother_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the female parent of the object.

niece_nephew_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the child of the object's sibling.
niece_nephew_of.derivation_rule: If A → parent_of → B and C → sibling_of → A, then B → niece_nephew_of → C

owns.description: Indicates that the subject possesses or has mastery over the object.

parent_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the mother or father of the object.

protector_of.description: Indicates that the subject defends or shields the object from harm.

protected_by.description: Indicates that the subject is defended or shielded by the object.

rival_of.description: Indicates that the subject competes with the object for the same goal, position, or affection.

ruler_of.description: Indicates that the subject holds authority or dominion over the object.

sacred_to.description: Indicates that the subject (e.g., a location or artifact) is dedicated to or holy to the object.

servant_of.description: Indicates that the subject serves, attends, or acts as a retainer to the object.

sibling_of.description: Indicates that the subject and object share one or more parents.

slays.description: Indicates that the subject killed or fatally destroyed the object.

son_in_law_of.description: Indicates that the subject is married to the object's daughter.

son_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the male offspring of the object.

spouse_of.description: Indicates a formal marital union between the subject and the object.

step_child_of.description: Indicates that the subject's parent is married to the object, who is not their biological parent.
step_child_of.derivation_rule: If A → parent_of → B and A → spouse_of → C, then B → step_child_of → C

step_parent_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the spouse of the object's parent but not their biological parent.
step_parent_of.derivation_rule: If A → spouse_of → B and B → parent_of → C, then A → step_parent_of → C

student_of.description: Indicates that the subject is taught or trained by the object.

suitor_of.description: Indicates that the subject actively pursued a romantic or marital union with the object.

teacher_of.description: Indicates that the subject instructs or trains the object.

twin_of.description: Indicates that the subject and object were born from the same pregnancy.

travels_to.description: Indicates that the subject journeys or has relocated to the object location.

uncle_aunt_of.description: Indicates that the subject is the sibling of the object's parent.
uncle_aunt_of.derivation_rule: If A → sibling_of → B and B → parent_of → C, then A → uncle_aunt_of → C

vassal_of.description: Indicates that the subject serves under or owes allegiance to the object in a feudal hierarchy.

visits.description: Indicates a temporary journey or stay by the subject at the object location.

ward_of.description: Indicates that the subject is under the care or protection of the object.
